metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define a concrete proving command for AC4 with a specific threshold (e.g. run `pytest tests/recall_regression.py` which replays N same-fingerprint pairs and asserts recall >= baseline + δ) so done-ness is deterministically verifiable.
findings.finding: AC4 ('Recall lift measured on same-material_fingerprint pairs') specifies no concrete proving command, no baseline, and no pass/fail threshold — it is outcome-vague and unverifiable as written.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, 4th bullet: 'Test: a finding present in a prior review but dropped on a re-run is re-surfaced; a resolved prior finding is dropped by Pass-2, not re-blocked. Recall lift measured on same-material_fingerprint pairs.'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'Recall lift measured on same-material_fingerprint pairs' with: 'Run `python scripts/measure_recall.py --pairs same_fingerprint_corpus/ --baseline recall_baseline.json`; assert recall_rate >= baseline + 0.05 on the blocking-criteria subset. Pass-2 drop of stale primed findings verified by the existing parity/isolation test asserting no stale norm_id survives Pass-2.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the mechanism conflict: either (a) demonstrate how prior findings can be injected into Pass-1 without breaking `test_prior_findings_only_reach_the_novelty_seam` (e.g. by scoping injection to a separate labelled block the test does not assert on), or (b) adopt the post-Pass-1 recall step as the primary approach and update the 'no extra LLM calls' AC to reflect the actual cost.
findings.finding: The plan's primary mechanism — injecting prior findings into Pass-1 as a context block — directly conflicts with the existing pinned independence test `test_prior_findings_only_reach_the_novelty_seam` (tests/unit/test_plan_review_novelty.py:67), which asserts 'independence by construction, not by prompt assertion' and would fail if the finder ever receives prior findings. The plan says the injection 'must keep the existing tests green' and names a post-Pass-1 recall step as fallback, but never resolves whether the primary approach can satisfy that constraint — leaving the implementation facing a silent break of a load-bearing invariant test.
findings.location: Plan § 'Structural-independence invariant (explicitly preserved)' and AC bullet 2 ('The existing finder-independence / parity-isolation test(s) still pass')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Identify and name the specific test(s) (file path + function name) that constitute the 'finder-independence / parity-isolation' guard, or, if they don't yet exist, note that they must be created as part of this work.
findings.finding: The plan references 'the existing finder-independence / parity-isolation test(s)' as the invariant guard, but no test by that name or description exists in the codebase. The closest tests are in tests/unit/workflow/test_plan_review_workflow.py and tests/unit/test_plan_review_assemble_memo.py, neither of which is labelled a 'finder-independence' or 'parity-isolation' test.
findings.location: Structural-independence invariant section / AC bullet 2: 'The existing finder-independence / parity-isolation test(s) still pass'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Identify and name the specific existing test(s) the 'finder-independence / parity-isolation' AC refers to (or acknowledge that the test must be created as part of this work and make that explicit in the AC).
findings.finding: The plan references 'the existing finder-independence / parity-isolation test(s)' as a named CI gate that must still pass, but no such test exists in the test suite under any discoverable variant of that name. The AC cannot be verified against a test that does not yet exist.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'The existing finder-independence / parity-isolation test(s) still pass'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'The existing finder-independence / parity-isolation test(s) still pass' with the specific test file and test name(s), e.g. 'tests/unit/test_review_kernel_rules.py::test_independence_instructions_never_leak_the_findings_conclusion still passes, AND a new test asserting the prior-findings block is labelled and not merged into the finding set is added.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Identify or create the specific test(s) that guard Pass-1 inter-chunk independence (the 'parity/isolation tests' the plan cites), name them explicitly, and confirm they would fail if prior findings leaked cross-chunk into Pass-1 finder context.
findings.finding: The plan's structural-independence safety argument rests on 'the plan-review parity/isolation test(s)' as a named, runnable invariant guard, but no such test exists in the codebase. The existing independence tests guard the novelty seam (`test_prior_findings_only_reach_the_novelty_seam`) and verifier conclusion non-leakage (`test_independence_instructions_never_leak_the_findings_conclusion`), not Pass-1 inter-chunk cross-contamination. The plan's conditional fallback ('IF the injection cannot be done without violating the parity/isolation test, the fallback is…') has no concrete, runnable trip-wire to evaluate.
findings.location: Structural-independence invariant section: 'finder independence (each finding/chunk judged without cross-contamination; guarded by the plan-review parity/isolation tests)'
findings.suggested_fix: Name the exact existing test(s) that guard the invariant (e.g. the novelty-seam test + a new test that asserts pass1_chunk's `instructions` parameter contains no prior-finding prose), or replace the vague 'parity/isolation tests' reference with a concrete assertion about which test file + function name serves as the trip-wire.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Identify (or create) the specific test(s) that guard Pass-1 finder inter-chunk independence and cite them by path/name in the Structural-independence invariant section, so the 'keep existing tests green' claim is verifiable.
findings.finding: The plan asserts finder independence is 'guarded by the plan-review parity/isolation test(s)' as a named, existing artifact — but no test bearing that description or guarding Pass-1 inter-chunk/cross-contamination independence can be found. The existing independence tests guard the novelty seam (`test_prior_findings_only_reach_the_novelty_seam`) and the verifier conclusion non-leakage (`test_independence_instructions_never_leak_the_findings_conclusion`); neither is the 'parity/isolation test' the plan cites as the invariant guard. If no such test exists, the plan's central safety argument ('IF the injection cannot be done without violating the parity/isolation test, the fallback is…') has no concrete trip-wire.
findings.location: Structural-independence invariant section: 'guarded by the plan-review parity/isolation tests'
findings.suggested_fix: Name the specific test file and test function(s) — e.g. `tests/unit/test_plan_review_novelty.py::test_prior_findings_only_reach_the_novelty_seam` and `tests/unit/test_review_kernel_rules.py::test_independence_instructions_never_leak_the_findings_conclusion` — or acknowledge that a new test is needed that asserts Pass-1 finder chunks receive no prior-finding content in their instructions, and add that test to scope.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reference and directly address ADR 0008's 'Pass-1 is unanchored' invariant: explain why injecting prior findings as a labelled context block does not re-introduce the documented anchoring/sycophancy risk, or propose an alternative mechanism (e.g. a post-Pass-1 recall step, which the plan already acknowledges as a fallback) that preserves the invariant.
findings.finding: The plan proposes injecting prior-review findings into the Pass-1 finder context, which directly contradicts the load-bearing invariant documented in ADR 0008 ('Pass-1 is unanchored. Every applicable criterion runs and the finder never sees the prior findings, so the anchoring/sycophancy the research documents cannot freeze a high-stakes defect an edit introduces.'). The plan neither cites ADR 0008 nor explains why its 'labelled external block' framing avoids the anchoring/sycophancy risk the ADR was designed to prevent.
findings.location: Plan body — 'What' section; 'Structural-independence invariant' section
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) restrict the recall enhancement to the post-Pass-1 fallback path the plan already acknowledges ('a separate post-Pass-1 recall step over prior norm_ids'), which preserves Pass-1's unanchored character and is structurally identical to the existing novelty sub-call pattern, or (b) cite the research the plan relies on to argue that labelled-context framing materially reduces anchoring vs unlabelled injection, add that evidence to the AC, and amend ADR 0008 to record the revised invariant.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly address ADR 0008 Invariant 1 ('Pass-1 is unanchored; the finder never sees prior findings'): either cite research demonstrating that a clearly-labelled external block avoids the documented anchoring/sycophancy risk, or propose formally superseding ADR 0008, or move the prior-findings injection to a post-Pass-1 recall step (the plan's own named fallback) that does not alter the Pass-1 finder context.
findings.finding: The plan's proposed Pass-1 injection directly contradicts ADR 0008 Invariant 1 — the load-bearing architectural decision that 'Pass-1 is unanchored. The finder never sees the prior findings' — without citing, superseding, or resolving that ADR. The `verify.py` comment (line 510–512) enforces this same invariant structurally by construction. Injecting prior findings into the Pass-1 finder as a 'clearly-delimited context block' is exactly the anchoring/sycophancy risk ADR 0008 was designed to prevent; labelling does not eliminate the LLM anchoring effect the ADR documented.
findings.location: Plan body — 'What' section: 'Feed this ticket's prior-review findings…into Pass-1 as clearly-delimited PRIOR-REVIEW CONCERNS'; Structural-independence invariant section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a positive rationale for the chosen approach (Pass-1 context injection) over the post-Pass-1 recall step, specifically addressing how it preserves the independence invariant and keeps existing tests green — OR promote the fallback to primary and justify why extra LLM calls are acceptable.
findings.finding: The plan does not provide a positive rationale for choosing context-injection into Pass-1 over the structurally different post-Pass-1 recall step (which it names as a fallback). A post-Pass-1 step that re-verifies missed prior norm_ids against the current plan would achieve the same recall lift with zero risk to the independence invariant and zero test-green risk. Without a rationale for why the primary approach is defensibly better on this dimension, the plan leaves the approach selection unjustified at the key trade-off.
findings.location: Plan § 'What' and § 'Structural-independence invariant (explicitly preserved)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State a concrete default threshold for the prime set (e.g. 'prior findings with validity ≥ 0.80 on blocking criteria E*/F* criteria') so the executor can act without clarification.
findings.finding: The scoping rule 'prior findings on blocking criteria that scored near/above the bar' uses 'near/above the bar' without defining a concrete score threshold or cutoff, leaving the executor to decide which prior findings to prime without guidance.
findings.location: Scope section: 'Scope the prime set to prior findings on blocking criteria that scored near/above the bar.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a concrete default, e.g.: 'Scope the prime set to prior findings on blocking criteria (E*, F*, S*) with a stored validity score ≥ 0.80, or all findings if validity is not stored.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define 'near/above the bar' concretely (e.g., a named score field, a numeric cutoff, or a configurable parameter) so the prime-set filter is deterministic, reproducible, and tunable.
findings.finding: The filter criterion 'prior findings on blocking criteria that scored near/above the bar' is asserted but left undefined — 'near the bar' has no specified numeric bound, scoring dimension, or configuration point, making the prime set non-deterministic and hard to tune or reproduce.
findings.location: Scope section: 'Scope the prime set to prior findings on blocking criteria that scored near/above the bar'
findings.suggested_fix: Specify the filter as a configurable parameter (e.g., `prime_min_score: float = 0.7`) referencing the same score field Pass-2 uses for validity, and document its default value and rationale.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify how norm_id is obtained on raw Pass-1 findings for the post-Pass-1 dedup step — either by calling sidecar.norm_id() on-the-fly (it's a pure function of the finding dict) or by naming a new enrichment step, and add this to the AC or implementation notes.
findings.finding: The plan says 'Dedup against the fresh findings by norm_id happens AFTER Pass-1,' but norm_id is a sidecar-only enrichment — raw Pass-1 advisory findings do NOT carry a norm_id field (confirmed by test_sidecar_enrichment.py line 69: `assert 'norm_id' not in verdict['advisory'][0]`). Deduplication by norm_id on the live findings stream requires either computing norm_id on-the-fly from the raw finding dict or a new pre-dedup enrichment step, neither of which is specified.
findings.location: What section / 'Dedup against the fresh findings by norm_id happens AFTER Pass-1'
findings.suggested_fix: Call `sidecar.norm_id(f)` on each fresh finding (the function is pure and already imported in the plan_review package) and compare against the set of norm_ids from the primed prior-findings block to perform dedup.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define a concrete, named threshold for the prime set scoping rule (e.g. 'prior findings with stored `validity` ≥ 0.80 on blocking criteria') so the filtering logic is unambiguous and testable.
findings.finding: The Scope section introduces a decision rule — 'scope the prime set to prior findings on blocking criteria that scored near/above the bar' — whose threshold vocabulary is never defined. 'Near/above the bar' is not a named score field, numeric value, or enum in the plan; the executor has no specified basis for inclusion/exclusion.
findings.location: Scope section: 'Scope the prime set to prior findings on blocking criteria that scored near/above the bar.'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'scored near/above the bar' with a concrete field-and-value specification, e.g.: 'prior findings on blocking criteria (E*, F*, S*) whose sidecar `validity` field is ≥ 0.80, or all prior findings if the validity field is absent.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an acceptance criterion (or extend AC1) that verifies the prime set is scoped to prior findings above a defined blocking/scoring threshold, so the filtering logic is testable.
findings.finding: The Scope section describes filtering the prime set to 'prior findings on blocking criteria that scored near/above the bar,' but no acceptance criterion verifies this filtering behavior — it is described work with no covering criterion.
findings.location: Scope section: 'Scope the prime set to prior findings on blocking criteria that scored near/above the bar.'
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC1: '…deduped by norm_id; the prime set is restricted to prior findings whose Pass-2 validity score met or exceeded [threshold]; they are NOT injected into the current finding set pre-verification.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the specific file(s) and function(s) that must be edited to inject the prior-findings context block (e.g., `passes.py:pass1_chunk` / `pass1.py:run_pass1`); confirm whether both the bespoke orchestrator path and the workflow runner path require changes.
findings.finding: The plan names no specific edit targets (files/functions/modules) for the 'Pass-1 context assembly' change. The actual injection point is passes.py:pass1_chunk() (builds the Pass-1 prompt via _resolve_system) and/or pass1.py:run_pass1(), but neither is named. Without named edit targets, implementors face ambiguity about where to inject the labelled prior-findings block.
findings.location: What section / 'Pass-1 context assembly + prior-sidecar load only'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how multi-chunk prior finding injection is handled: (a) how chunks are selected for injection (only the chunk whose criteria best match the prior finding's criteria?), (b) the dedup resolution strategy when two chunks both re-surface the same prior finding with slightly different wording, and (c) what the `crit` filter in `pass1_chunk` does when a re-surfaced prior is tagged with out-of-chunk criteria.
findings.finding: The plan does not handle the multi-chunk duplication edge case: a prior finding tagged with criteria that span multiple chunks (e.g., a finding for both G1 and G2, which may land in different chunks) will be injected into each applicable chunk. If the model re-surfaces it from two chunks, the post-Pass-1 `norm_id` dedup must collapse them — but the plan does not specify which version to keep (the one with the most evidence? the first? the highest-confidence?), nor what happens if the per-chunk `crit` filter in `pass1_chunk` silently drops a re-surfaced prior finding because the model re-tagged it with criteria outside the chunk's rubric.
findings.location: Plan § 'What' (dedup mechanism) and AC bullet 1

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Capture the prior-finding priming architecture (rationale, independence invariant, fallback strategy) in an ADR or CLAUDE.md update so future maintainers have a durable design record.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a materially new architectural pattern — injecting prior-review memory into a previously stateless Pass-1 finder — but does not call for capturing this decision in an ADR, CLAUDE.md, or design-doc update.
findings.location: Structural-independence invariant section / overall plan
findings.suggested_fix: Add a brief ADR (or CLAUDE.md section) that records: (1) the recall problem motivating the change, (2) how structural independence is preserved (labelled external context, Pass-2 verification backstop), and (3) the fallback if parity/isolation tests would be broken.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define and implement a graceful-degradation path for sidecar load failures: specify that Pass-1 proceeds with an empty prior-findings set (normal operation) when the corpus is missing, unreadable, or yields no norm_id matches, and surface a warning log for each failure mode.
findings.finding: The plan specifies no error-handling or graceful-degradation path for sidecar corpus load failures (file missing, malformed data, norm_id match errors). If the load fails, it is unspecified whether Pass-1 aborts entirely, proceeds without priming, or surfaces an error — leaving a new failure point that could silently degrade recall or crash the pipeline.
findings.location: ## What — 'Feed this ticket\'s prior-review findings (from the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar corpus, matched by `norm_id`) into Pass-1'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an explicit policy statement: 'If the sidecar corpus is unavailable, unreadable, or yields no matching norm_ids, Pass-1 proceeds normally (no priming); a WARNING log entry is emitted with the failure reason. No exception propagates to the caller.' Ensure the sidecar load is wrapped in a try/except with this fallback in the implementation spec.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an observability AC: specify that each Pass-1 invocation logs (at minimum) the count of prior findings loaded, the count matched by norm_id, and any load/match errors, so operators can verify the recall-injection path is functioning.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new operational path (sidecar load + norm_id matching + context injection) but specifies no observability instrumentation — no logs, metrics, or traces for the new failure points, making it impossible for operators to detect when priming is silently skipped or producing unexpected results.
findings.location: ## What and ## Acceptance Criteria — no observability AC present
findings.suggested_fix: Add AC: 'Pass-1 emits a structured log entry per invocation containing: ticket_id, prior_findings_loaded (int), prior_findings_matched (int), load_error (str|null). A dashboard/alert monitors load_error rate and prior_findings_matched == 0 rate.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a citation or reference to the data/experiment that supports the claim that labelled prior-finding injection at Pass-1 specifically closes the 14/17-flip miss gap, or downgrade the assertion to a hypothesis and add a measurement AC that gates the feature on observed recall lift.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that prior-finding context injection into Pass-1 will lift recall ('14/17 flips were because the other run simply MISSED the valid finding') but provides no citation or experiment reference for the claim that this specific mechanism — as opposed to, e.g., repeated sampling, model upgrade, or the post-Pass-1 recall step — closes the gap.
findings.location: Plan body — 'Why' section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit fallback policy for sidecar corpus load failures: specify that Pass-1 proceeds normally with an empty prior-findings set (no priming) when the corpus is absent, unreadable, or yields no norm_id matches, and emit a WARNING log entry for each failure mode.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new sidecar corpus load path ('Feed this ticket's prior-review findings from the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar corpus, matched by norm_id') but specifies no fallback for failure modes: missing corpus (first-run ticket with no prior review), malformed/unreadable sidecar data, or norm_id matching that yields zero results. The only fallback the plan names ('a separate post-Pass-1 recall step') is conditioned on a parity-test violation, not sidecar unavailability.
findings.location: ## What section: 'Feed this ticket's prior-review findings (from the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar corpus, matched by `norm_id`) into Pass-1'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the plan: 'If the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar is absent, unreadable, or yields no norm_id matches for the ticket, Pass-1 proceeds as a standard (unprimed) run. No exception propagates; a WARNING log entry records the reason. This is the expected behavior for a first-run ticket.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define 'material_fingerprint' in the plan body (or a Definitions section) and ensure AC4's verify step references that definition so the recall-lift measurement is unambiguous.
findings.finding: The term 'material_fingerprint' is introduced in AC4 ('Recall lift measured on same-`material_fingerprint` pairs') without definition anywhere in the plan body, creating a terminology gap that leaves the test criterion ambiguous.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC4: 'Recall lift measured on same-`material_fingerprint` pairs.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the plan: 'material_fingerprint: a stable hash of the review material (e.g., plan text + rubric ids) used to identify re-runs over identical inputs.' Then reference this definition in AC4.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
